name: finance-vix
title: VIX - CBOE Volatility Index
description: Daily and monthly time-series of the CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) from January 1990 to present, including open, high, low, and close values. VIX measures the market's expectation of 30-day S&P 500 volatility derived from options prices and is widely known as the 'fear gauge'.

licenses.name: ODC-PDDL-1.0
licenses.title: Open Data Commons Public Domain Dedication and License v1.0

sources.name: CBOE VIX Historical Data
sources.title: CBOE VIX Daily Price History CSV

views.name: vix-fear-index
views.title: VIX Fear Index (1990–present)
views.description: The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) measures expected 30-day volatility of the S&P 500, derived from options prices — widely called the 'fear gauge'. Below 20 signals complacency; above 30 indicates fear; above 40 marks extreme panic. Historic spikes: 1998 (LTCM/Russia crisis ~45), 2008 financial crisis (peak 80), 2020 COVID crash (peak 82).

resources.name: vix-monthly
resources.description: Month-end VIX closing values aggregated from the daily series. Note: this file is not updated automatically by the workflow and may be stale.

resources.name: vix-daily
resources.description: Daily VIX open, high, low, and close values from January 1990 to present. Rows through 06/11/2004 (506 rows) have identical OPEN, HIGH, LOW, and CLOSE values because CBOE only recorded closing prices before that date.

fields.name: OPEN
fields.description: VIX opening value. Identical to CLOSE for dates on or before 06/11/2004.

fields.name: HIGH
fields.description: VIX intraday high. Identical to CLOSE for dates on or before 06/11/2004.

fields.name: LOW
fields.description: VIX intraday low. Identical to CLOSE for dates on or before 06/11/2004.
